BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".

- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.

1	"""Data-sovereignty helper: enforce that network endpoints are loopback-only (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Every engine component that takes a host/URL for an on-box service (local model runtime,
4	tracing collector, vector store, checkpoint database) routes it through here so a non-local
5	address fails loud rather than silently shipping data off the machine.
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	import ipaddress
11	import re
12	from urllib.parse import parse_qs, urlparse
13	
14	# A libpq keyword/value token: ``key = value`` with optional whitespace around ``=`` (libpq
15	# permits it) and an optionally single/double-quoted value (which may contain spaces).
16	_DSN_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"(\w+)\s*=\s*('[^']*'|\"[^\"]*\"|\S+)")
17	
18	
19	class NonLocalEndpointError(RuntimeError):
20	    """Raised when a configured endpoint is not loopback (would leave the machine)."""
21	
22	
23	def is_loopback_host(host: str | None) -> bool:
24	    """True only for genuine loopback hosts.
25	
26	    Parses the host as an IP and tests the loopback network (127.0.0.0/8, ::1), so a public
27	    name that merely *starts with* ``127.`` (e.g. ``127.0.0.1.evil.com``) is correctly
28	    rejected. The literal ``localhost`` is the only accepted non-IP host.
29	    """
30	    if not host:
31	        return False
32	    h = host.strip().lower()
33	    if h == "localhost":
34	        return True
35	    try:
36	        return ipaddress.ip_address(h.strip("[]")).is_loopback
37	    except ValueError:
38	        return False
39	
40	
41	def require_loopback_url(url: str, *, what: str) -> str:
42	    """Return ``url`` if its host is loopback; else raise :class:`NonLocalEndpointError`."""
43	    host = urlparse(url).hostname
44	    if not is_loopback_host(host):
45	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
46	            f"{what} must be loopback-only (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); got host {host!r} "
47	            f"from {url!r}"
48	        )
49	    return url
50	
51	
52	def require_loopback_host(host: str, *, what: str) -> str:
53	    """Return ``host`` if it is loopback; else raise. (``0.0.0.0`` binds all interfaces and
54	    is correctly rejected — it would expose data off-box.)"""
55	    if not is_loopback_host(host):
56	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
57	            f"{what} must be a loopback host (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); got {host!r}"
58	        )
59	    return host
60	
61	
62	def _authority_hosts(netloc: str) -> list[str]:
63	    """Every host in a URI authority, supporting libpq's comma-separated multi-host form.
64	
65	    ``urlparse().hostname`` returns only the *first* host of a multi-host authority
66	    (``host1:port1,host2:port2``), so a remote second host would slip past a single-host
67	    check (audit #D3). Each host may carry its own ``:port`` and IPv6 hosts are bracketed.
68	
69	    Userinfo is stripped at the **first** ``@`` — matching libpq, which (unlike ``urlparse``,
70	    which uses the last ``@``) treats the first ``@`` as the userinfo delimiter. A string like
71	    ``[REDACTED]:5432@localhost`` is therefore read as host ``evil.com`` here, not
72	    ``localhost`` (audit #D5). Any host element still containing ``@`` after that is ambiguous
73	    and is returned verbatim so the caller's loopback check fails it closed.
74	    """
75	    if "@" in netloc:
76	        netloc = netloc.split("@", 1)[1]
77	    hosts: list[str] = []
78	    for part in netloc.split(","):
79	        p = part.strip()
80	        if not p:
81	            continue
82	        if p.startswith("["):  # [ipv6] or [ipv6]:port
83	            end = p.find("]")
84	            hosts.append(p[1:end] if end != -1 else p[1:])
85	        else:
86	            hosts.append(p.rsplit(":", 1)[0] if ":" in p else p)
87	    return hosts
88	
89	
90	def require_local_uri(uri: str, *, what: str) -> str:
91	    """Return ``uri`` if it is local (ADR-0003), else raise :class:`NonLocalEndpointError`.
92	
93	    Three forms are recognized, validated against what the real client (libpq for postgres,
94	    urllib for http, sqlite for files) actually connects to — not merely what ``urlparse``
95	    reports, since the two can disagree:
96	
97	    - **URI with an authority** (``scheme://host…`` or scheme-relative ``//host…``): *every*
98	      host in the authority must be loopback (incl. each of a comma-separated multi-host
99	      authority, #D3), AND any ``host``/``hostaddr`` query parameter that libpq would use to
100	      override the authority host (#D2). ``hostaddr`` is checked independently of ``host``.
101	    - **libpq keyword/value DSN** (no authority but ``key=value`` tokens): ``host``/``hostaddr``
102	      must be loopback, tolerating libpq's whitespace/quoting around ``=`` (#P1, #D1).
103	    - **bare path** (no authority, no ``=``): a local file/dir — but a Windows UNC path
104	      (``\\\\server\\share``) is rejected as remote.
105	
106	    Inputs that cannot be parsed safely fail closed (rejected), never accepted.
107	    """
108	    try:
109	        parsed = urlparse(uri)
110	        netloc = parsed.netloc
111	        query = parsed.query
112	    except ValueError:
113	        # e.g. a malformed IPv6 authority — we cannot determine the host, so refuse.
114	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
115	            f"{what}: endpoint could not be parsed safely; rejecting (ADR-0003)"
116	        ) from None
117	
118	    if netloc:
119	        # A backslash never legitimately appears in a URI authority; a WHATWG-normalizing
120	        # client (e.g. some HTTP stacks) treats '\' as '/', so it could read a different host
121	        # than urlparse. Refuse rather than risk that differential (audit #D6, defense-in-depth).
122	        if "\\" in netloc:
123	            raise NonLocalEndpointError(
124	                f"{what}: backslash in URI authority is not permitted (ADR-0003); rejecting"
125	            )
126	        for h in _authority_hosts(netloc):
127	            # A residual '@' means the authority is ambiguous between this parser and libpq —
128	            # fail closed (audit #D5).
129	            if "@" in h or not is_loopback_host(h):
130	                raise NonLocalEndpointError(
131	                    f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); authority "
132	                    f"names non-loopback host {h!r}"
133	                )
134	        for key in ("host", "hostaddr"):
135	            for val in parse_qs(query).get(key, []):
136	                h = val.strip()
137	                if h and not is_loopback_host(h):
138	                    raise NonLocalEndpointError(
139	                        f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); URI query "
140	                        f"{key}={h!r} points off-box"
141	                    )
142	        return uri
143	
144	    if "=" in uri:  # libpq keyword/value DSN (no URI authority)
145	        for match in _DSN_TOKEN_RE.finditer(uri):
146	            if match.group(1).strip().lower() in {"host", "hostaddr"}:
147	                h = match.group(2).strip().strip("'\"")
148	                if h and not is_loopback_host(h):
149	                    raise NonLocalEndpointError(
150	                        f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); DSN names "
151	                        f"non-loopback host {h!r}"
152	                    )
153	        return uri
154	
155	    stripped = uri.lstrip()
156	    # Windows treats ANY two leading separators (in any mix of '\' and '/') as a UNC prefix
157	    # to a remote SMB share — \\srv, //srv, /\srv, \/srv all resolve off-box (audit #D6).
158	    # (The pure '//host' form is already caught by the authority branch above; covering it
159	    # here too keeps the branch self-contained.)
160	    if len(stripped) >= 2 and stripped[0] in "\\/" and stripped[1] in "\\/":
161	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
162	            f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); UNC-style path "
163	            f"{uri!r} is a remote share"
164	        )
165	    return uri  # bare path / dir store
166

1	"""Experiment tracking — a local, on-box run/metric/artifact store (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's MLflow setup. The backend is a local store
4	(default: a SQLite file under the working directory); a remote tracking server is rejected by
5	the local-URI guard. MLflow is optional (the ``observability`` extra) and imported lazily, so
6	this module loads without it.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import os
12	from pathlib import Path
13	from typing import Any, Mapping, Protocol, runtime_checkable
14	
15	from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
16	
17	_DEFAULT_URI = "sqlite:///assay_mlflow.db"
18	EXPERIMENT_NAME = os.environ.get("ASSAY_EXPERIMENT", "assay")
19	
20	
21	def get_tracking_uri() -> str:
22	    """Resolve the tracking URI (env override, else local SQLite), enforced local."""
23	    uri = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACKING_URI", _DEFAULT_URI)
24	    # Resolve a relative sqlite path to an absolute file so runs land deterministically.
25	    prefix = "sqlite:///"
26	    if uri.startswith(prefix):
27	        rel = uri[len(prefix) :]
28	        if not os.path.isabs(rel):
29	            uri = prefix + str((Path.cwd() / rel).resolve())
30	    return require_local_uri(uri, what="experiment tracking")
31	
32	
33	@runtime_checkable
34	class ExperimentTracker(Protocol):
35	    def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str: ...
36	    def log_metric(self, run_id: str, key: str, value: float) -> None: ...
37	    def log_artifact(self, run_id: str, path: str) -> None: ...
38	    def end_run(self, run_id: str) -> None: ...
39	
40	
41	class MlflowExperimentTracker:
42	    """A local-store experiment tracker backed by MLflow (lazy import).
43	
44	    Construction validates the tracking URI is local and selects the experiment; it does not
45	    import MLflow until a run is started, so the module is importable without the extra.
46	    """
47	
48	    def __init__(self, experiment: str = EXPERIMENT_NAME) -> None:
49	        self._experiment = experiment
50	        self._uri = get_tracking_uri()  # validates local before anything else
51	
52	    def _client(self) -> Any:
53	        """An MlflowClient bound to the local store. Uses the client API (keyed by run_id)
54	        throughout, so no run ever sits on the global active-run stack — start_run followed by
55	        log_metric cannot collide (audit issue #O1)."""
56	        try:
57	            from mlflow.tracking import MlflowClient
58	        except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
59	            raise RuntimeError(
60	                "experiment tracking requires the 'observability' extra (mlflow) — not installed"
61	            ) from exc
62	        return MlflowClient(tracking_uri=self._uri)
63	
64	    def _experiment_id(self, client: Any) -> str:
65	        exp = client.get_experiment_by_name(self._experiment)
66	        if exp is not None:
67	            return str(exp.experiment_id)
68	        return str(client.create_experiment(self._experiment))
69	
70	    def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str:
71	        client = self._client()
72	        run = client.create_run(self._experiment_id(client), run_name=name)
73	        run_id = str(run.info.run_id)
74	        for k, v in dict(params).items():
75	            client.log_param(run_id, k, v)
76	        return run_id
77	
78	    def log_metric(self, run_id: str, key: str, value: float) -> None:
79	        self._client().log_metric(run_id, key, value)
80	
81	    def log_artifact(self, run_id: str, path: str) -> None:
82	        self._client().log_artifact(run_id, path)
83	
84	    def end_run(self, run_id: str) -> None:
85	        self._client().set_terminated(run_id)
86

1	"""Tiered reasoning seam — the single abstraction over LLM execution by stakes tier.
2	
3	No engine component talks to an LLM directly; everything routes through :class:`ReasoningSeam`.
4	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's hardened ``reasoning_client`` (ARCHITECTURE.md
5	§1, ADR-0003). Two execution tiers:
6	
7	- ``StakesTier.BULK`` — a local model runtime (loopback-only, e.g. Ollama) for high-volume,
8	  low-stakes work.
9	- ``StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES`` — a frontier model via a **fixed-cost subscription** path
10	  (the ``claude`` CLI / Agent SDK using an OAuth token). No metered API key is ever used:
11	  every metered Anthropic credential is scrubbed from the subprocess environment first.
12	
13	Hardened invariants preserved from the prior platform:
14	
15	- forward-proof credential scrubbing (any ``ANTHROPIC_*KEY``/``*TOKEN`` stripped; the
16	  subscription ``CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`` preserved);
17	- hard per-call timeouts via a bounded worker pool with a saturation guard;
18	- independent retry budgets — exponential backoff for transient errors, long backpressure
19	  for rate limits — and permanent errors never retried;
20	- robust JSON extraction via a balanced-brace walker (not a greedy regex);
21	- loopback enforcement on the local backend (ADR-0003).
22	
23	Heavy backends (``langchain_ollama``, ``claude_agent_sdk``) and tracing (``opentelemetry``)
24	are imported lazily, so this module imports — and is unit-testable by mocking ``_attempt`` —
25	with none of them installed. Install the optional ``reasoning`` extra to run real calls.
26	"""
27	
28	from __future__ import annotations
29	
30	import json
31	import os
32	import threading
33	import time
34	from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
35	from concurrent.futures import TimeoutError as FuturesTimeout
36	from contextlib import contextmanager
37	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
38	from enum import Enum
39	from pathlib import Path
40	from typing import Any, Callable, Iterator, Mapping, Protocol, cast, runtime_checkable
41	
42	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
43	from assay_engine._local import require_loopback_url
44	
45	
46	class StakesTier(Enum):
47	    BULK = "bulk"  # local model — high volume, low stakes
48	    HIGH_STAKES = "high"  # frontier model via fixed-cost subscription — gated, traced
49	
50	
51	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
52	class ReasoningRequest:
53	    prompt: str
54	    tier: StakesTier
55	    purpose: str
56	    params: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
57	
58	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
59	        object.__setattr__(self, "params", freeze_mapping(self.params))
60	
61	
62	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
63	# Errors
64	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
65	class ReasoningError(RuntimeError):
66	    """Transient reasoning failure (eligible for retry)."""
67	
68	
69	class PermanentReasoningError(ReasoningError):
70	    """Non-transient failure — never retried (bad model, auth, saturation)."""
71	
72	
73	class RateLimitError(ReasoningError):
74	    """Rate-limit / capacity signal — retried with long backpressure, not fast backoff."""
75	
76	
77	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
78	# Config (dataset-agnostic env contract, ASSAY_* prefix)
79	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
80	def _env(name: str, default: str) -> str:
81	    val = os.environ.get(name)
82	    return val if val is not None and val != "" else default
83	
84	
85	def _truthy(name: str) -> bool:
86	    return os.environ.get(name, "").strip().lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}
87	
88	
89	BULK_MODEL = _env("ASSAY_BULK_MODEL", "llama3.1:8b")
90	BULK_BASE_URL = _env("ASSAY_BULK_BASE_URL", "http://localhost:11434")
91	HIGH_STAKES_MODEL = os.environ.get("ASSAY_HIGH_STAKES_MODEL") or None
92	
93	BULK_TIMEOUT = max(1.0, float(_env("ASSAY_BULK_TIMEOUT", "120")))
94	# Cap BULK generation length. ChatOllama/Ollama default to unbounded (-1); without a cap a
95	# local model can run away to context exhaustion, and the wall-clock timeout cannot interrupt a
96	# call already blocked in the backend (a leaked pool slot). Bounding output tokens is the real
97	# guard (LangChain's own ChatOllama example sets num_predict explicitly).
98	BULK_NUM_PREDICT = max(1, int(_env("ASSAY_BULK_NUM_PREDICT", "2048")))
99	HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT = max(1.0, float(_env("ASSAY_HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT", "300")))
100	
101	MAX_RETRIES = max(0, int(_env("ASSAY_REASONING_RETRIES", "2")))
102	BACKOFF_BASE = max(0.0, float(_env("ASSAY_REASONING_BACKOFF", "1.5")))
103	RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF = max(0.0, float(_env("ASSAY_RATELIMIT_BACKOFF", "30")))
104	RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES = max(0, int(_env("ASSAY_RATELIMIT_RETRIES", "5")))
105	
106	# OAuth subscription token name (external CLI contract — not renamed).
107	OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV = "CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN"
108	
109	
110	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
111	# Credential scrubbing (ADR-0003 / no metered API) — pure, forward-proof
112	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
113	def is_metered_anthropic_credential(key: str) -> bool:
114	    """True for any env var the Anthropic SDK would read as a METERED credential.
115	
116	    Matches the *shape* — any ``ANTHROPIC_*`` var naming a ``KEY`` or ``TOKEN`` — rather than
117	    a static list, so a newly-added metered var cannot silently slip through. The subscription
118	    ``CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`` does not start with ``ANTHROPIC_`` and is deliberately kept.
119	    """
120	    return key.startswith("ANTHROPIC_") and ("KEY" in key or "TOKEN" in key)
121	
122	
123	def scrubbed_env() -> dict[str, str]:
124	    """The process env with every metered Anthropic credential removed.
125	
126	    Execution must run on the fixed subscription via the CLI's OAuth token; a metered key, if
127	    present, would silently bill per token — so strip it before the SDK subprocess sees it.
128	    """
129	    return {k: v for k, v in os.environ.items() if not is_metered_anthropic_credential(k)}
130	
131	
132	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
133	# Bounded timeout pool with saturation guard
134	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
135	_POOL_WORKERS = 8
136	_pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=_POOL_WORKERS, thread_name_prefix="reasoning-timeout")
137	_inflight = 0
138	_inflight_lock = threading.Lock()
139	
140	
141	def _release(_future: Any) -> None:
142	    global _inflight
143	    with _inflight_lock:
144	        _inflight -= 1
145	
146	
147	def _submit_bounded(fn: Callable[[], Any]) -> Any:
148	    """Submit to the shared pool, refusing if every worker slot is already in flight
149	    (hung backend calls would otherwise leak all slots)."""
150	    global _inflight
151	    with _inflight_lock:
152	        if _inflight >= _POOL_WORKERS:
153	            raise PermanentReasoningError(
154	                f"reasoning timeout pool saturated ({_inflight}/{_POOL_WORKERS} in-flight) — "
155	                "hung backend call(s) have leaked every worker slot"
156	            )
157	        _inflight += 1
158	    try:
159	        future = _pool.submit(fn)
160	    except BaseException:
161	        with _inflight_lock:
162	            _inflight -= 1
163	        raise
164	    future.add_done_callback(_release)
165	    return future
166	
167	
168	def _with_timeout(fn: Callable[[], Any], timeout: float, what: str) -> Any:
169	    """Run a sync callable with a hard wall-clock timeout via the bounded pool."""
170	    future = _submit_bounded(fn)
171	    try:
172	        return future.result(timeout=timeout)
173	    except FuturesTimeout as exc:
174	        # No future.cancel(): a ThreadPoolExecutor cannot interrupt a thread already blocked
175	        # in a backend call. The worker keeps its slot until the call returns; the bounded
176	        # pool's saturation guard is the real protection against leaked/hung slots.
177	        raise ReasoningError(f"{what} timed out after {timeout:.0f}s") from exc
178	
179	
180	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
181	# JSON extraction — balanced-brace walker (robust to fences / trailing prose)
182	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
183	def _first_balanced_json(text: str) -> str | None:
184	    """Return the first balanced ``{...}`` or ``[...]`` span, respecting string literals.
185	
186	    A greedy regex grabs from the first brace to the LAST brace, mangling replies with
187	    multiple JSON values or braces in trailing prose; this walks the structure honestly.
188	    """
189	    start = None
190	    opener = closer = ""
191	    depth = 0
192	    in_string = False
193	    escape = False
194	    for i, ch in enumerate(text):
195	        if start is None:
196	            if ch in "{[":
197	                start, opener = i, ch
198	                closer = "}" if ch == "{" else "]"
199	                depth = 1
200	            continue
201	        if in_string:
202	            if escape:
203	                escape = False
204	            elif ch == "\\":
205	                escape = True
206	            elif ch == '"':
207	                in_string = False
208	            continue
209	        if ch == '"':
210	            in_string = True
211	        elif ch == opener:
212	            depth += 1
213	        elif ch == closer:
214	            depth -= 1
215	            if depth == 0:
216	                return text[start : i + 1]
217	    return None
218	
219	
220	def extract_json(text: str) -> Any:
221	    """Parse JSON from a model reply (handles code fences and surrounding prose)."""
222	    span = _first_balanced_json(text)
223	    if span is None:
224	        raise ReasoningError("no JSON object/array found in reply")
225	    try:
226	        return json.loads(span)
227	    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
228	        raise ReasoningError(f"reply contained malformed JSON: {exc}") from exc
229	
230	
231	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
232	# Tier backends (lazy imports — module loads without them)
233	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
234	def _bulk_complete(
235	    prompt: str,
236	    system: str | None,
237	    temperature: float,
238	    model: str,
239	    json_mode: bool = False,
240	    *,
241	    seed: int | None = None,
242	    json_schema: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
243	) -> str:
244	    """Tier: BULK — local model runtime (loopback-enforced).
245	
246	    ``num_predict`` is always set (bounded generation). For JSON, a caller-supplied
247	    ``json_schema`` uses Ollama's schema-constrained decoding (the reliable path); otherwise
248	    ``json_mode`` falls back to loose ``format="json"``. ``seed`` lets a retry force a different
249	    deterministic decode without sharpening temperature.
250	    """
251	    require_loopback_url(BULK_BASE_URL, what="bulk-tier model base URL")
252	    try:
253	        from langchain_ollama import ChatOllama
254	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
255	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
256	            "bulk tier requires the 'reasoning' extra (langchain-ollama) — not installed"
257	        ) from exc
258	
259	    kwargs: dict[str, Any] = dict(
260	        model=model,
261	        base_url=BULK_BASE_URL,
262	        temperature=temperature,
263	        num_predict=BULK_NUM_PREDICT,  # bound generation length (H1)
264	        client_kwargs={"timeout": BULK_TIMEOUT},
265	    )
266	    if seed is not None:
267	        kwargs["seed"] = seed
268	    if json_schema is not None:
269	        kwargs["format"] = json_schema  # Ollama schema-constrained decoding (reliable JSON)
270	    elif json_mode:
271	        kwargs["format"] = "json"  # loose native JSON decoding (syntactic only)
272	    client = ChatOllama(**kwargs)
273	    messages: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
274	    if system:
275	        messages.append(("system", system))
276	    messages.append(("human", prompt))
277	    try:
278	        reply = client.invoke(messages)
279	    except Exception as exc:
280	        # Prefer the typed HTTP status (ollama.ResponseError / httpx carry one): 404 = the model
281	        # isn't pulled → permanent. Fall back to substring matching only if no status is exposed
282	        # (robust to upstream message-wording changes — audit M4).
283	        status = getattr(exc, "status_code", None) or getattr(
284	            getattr(exc, "response", None), "status_code", None
285	        )
286	        msg = str(exc).lower()
287	        permanent = status == 404 or any(
288	            s in msg for s in ("not found", "no such model", "try pulling")
289	        )
290	        if permanent:
291	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"bulk model {model!r} not available: {exc}") from exc
292	        raise ReasoningError(f"bulk tier call failed: {exc}") from exc
293	    content = getattr(reply, "content", reply)
294	    text = content if isinstance(content, str) else str(content)
295	    text = text.strip()
296	    if not text:  # an empty reply is not a valid result — retry, don't return "" (issue #5/R5)
297	        raise ReasoningError("bulk tier returned an empty reply")
298	    return text
299	
300	
301	def _high_stakes_auth_present() -> bool:
302	    """Subscription auth available: an exported OAuth token OR a stored CLI credentials file
303	    (the normal state after ``claude login`` without exporting a token)."""
304	    if os.environ.get(OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV):
305	        return True
306	    return (Path.home() / ".claude" / ".credentials.json").is_file()
307	
308	
309	def _high_stakes_complete(prompt: str, system: str | None, model: str | None) -> str:
310	    """Tier: HIGH_STAKES — frontier model via subscription CLI/Agent SDK (no metered key)."""
311	    try:
312	        import anyio
313	        from claude_agent_sdk import (
314	            AssistantMessage,
315	            ClaudeAgentOptions,
316	            ResultMessage,
317	            TextBlock,
318	            query,
319	        )
320	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
321	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
322	            "high-stakes tier requires the 'reasoning' extra (claude-agent-sdk) — not installed"
323	        ) from exc
324	    if not _high_stakes_auth_present():
325	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
326	            f"high-stakes tier requires subscription auth ({OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV} or a stored "
327	            "~/.claude/.credentials.json); none found"
328	        )
329	
330	    options = ClaudeAgentOptions(
331	        system_prompt=system,
332	        model=model,
333	        permission_mode="dontAsk",
334	        setting_sources=[],
335	        allowed_tools=[],
336	        env=scrubbed_env(),  # ADR-0003: never pass a metered Anthropic credential
337	    )
338	
339	    parts: list[str] = []
340	    result_error: dict[str, Any] | None = None
341	
342	    async def _drain() -> None:
343	        # The CLI/SDK signals rate windows and API failures by COMPLETING the stream
344	        # (a RateLimitEvent, or a terminal ResultMessage with is_error) — NOT by raising.
345	        # We must inspect the stream, not just catch exceptions (issue #R4).
346	        nonlocal result_error
347	        async for msg in query(prompt=prompt, options=options):
348	            if type(msg).__name__ == "RateLimitEvent":
349	                rl = getattr(getattr(msg, "rate_limit_info", None), "status", None)
350	                if rl == "rejected":
351	                    result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "RateLimitEvent rejected"}
352	                continue
353	            if getattr(msg, "status", None) == "rejected":
354	                result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "rejected"}
355	                continue
356	            if isinstance(msg, AssistantMessage):
357	                for block in getattr(msg, "content", []) or []:
358	                    if isinstance(block, TextBlock):
359	                        parts.append(block.text)
360	            elif isinstance(msg, ResultMessage):
361	                # Do not let a trailing ResultMessage clobber an already-captured 429.
362	                if getattr(msg, "is_error", False) and not (
363	                    result_error and result_error.get("status") == 429
364	                ):
365	                    result_error = {
366	                        "status": getattr(msg, "api_error_status", None),
367	                        "detail": getattr(msg, "errors", None) or getattr(msg, "stop_reason", None),
368	                    }
369	
370	    try:
371	        anyio.run(_drain)
372	    except Exception as exc:
373	        raise ReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier call failed: {exc}") from exc
374	
375	    if result_error is not None:
376	        status = result_error.get("status")
377	        detail = result_error.get("detail") or status
378	        if status in (429, 529):
379	            raise RateLimitError(f"high-stakes tier rate window ({status}): {detail}")
380	        if status in (401, 403):
381	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier auth failed ({status}): {detail}")
382	        raise ReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier reported an error (status={status}): {detail}")
383	
384	    text = "".join(parts).strip()
385	    if not text:  # empty frontier reply is not a valid result — retry (issue #R5)
386	        raise ReasoningError("high-stakes tier returned an empty reply")
387	    return text
388	
389	
390	def _attempt(request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
391	    """Route one request to its tier backend (no retry here)."""
392	    if _truthy("ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING"):
393	        raise ReasoningError("reasoning is disabled (ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING)")
394	    p = request.params
395	    system = p.get("system")
396	    temperature = float(p.get("temperature", 0.0))
397	    model = p.get("model")
398	    json_mode = bool(p.get("_json_mode", False))
399	    seed = p.get("_seed")
400	    json_schema = p.get("json_schema")  # optional caller schema → constrained JSON decoding
401	    if request.tier is StakesTier.BULK:
402	        return cast(
403	            str,
404	            _with_timeout(
405	                lambda: _bulk_complete(
406	                    request.prompt,
407	                    system,
408	                    temperature,
409	                    model or BULK_MODEL,
410	                    json_mode,
411	                    seed=int(seed) if seed is not None else None,
412	                    json_schema=json_schema,
413	                ),
414	                BULK_TIMEOUT + 10,
415	                "bulk tier",
416	            ),
417	        )
418	    if request.tier is StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES:
419	        # The high-stakes tier runs the Claude CLI subprocess (not the in-process Anthropic SDK,
420	        # so no auto-instrumentor covers it) — emit an explicit OpenInference LLM span so Phoenix
421	        # records the model/provider for this otherwise-untraced tier (#2).
422	        llm_attrs = {
423	            "llm.provider": "anthropic",
424	            "llm.system": "anthropic",
425	            "llm.model_name": str(model or HIGH_STAKES_MODEL or "subscription-default"),
426	        }
427	        with _span("reasoning.high_stakes", llm_attrs, kind="LLM"):
428	            return cast(
429	                str,
430	                _with_timeout(
431	                    lambda: _high_stakes_complete(
432	                        request.prompt, system, model or HIGH_STAKES_MODEL
433	                    ),
434	                    HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT + 30,
435	                    "high-stakes tier",
436	                ),
437	            )
438	    raise PermanentReasoningError(f"unknown tier: {request.tier!r}")
439	
440	
441	def _run_with_retries(request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
442	    """Execute with independent transient and rate-limit retry budgets."""
443	    transient = 0
444	    rate = 0
445	    while True:
446	        try:
447	            return _attempt(request)
448	        except PermanentReasoningError:
449	            raise
450	        except RateLimitError:
451	            if rate >= RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES:
452	                raise
453	            time.sleep(RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF * (rate + 1))
454	            rate += 1
455	        except ReasoningError:
456	            if transient >= MAX_RETRIES:
457	                raise
458	            time.sleep(BACKOFF_BASE**transient)
459	            transient += 1
460	
461	
462	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
463	# Public seam
464	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
465	@runtime_checkable
466	class ReasoningSeam(Protocol):
467	    """Route a reasoning request to the appropriate tier with timeouts, retries, tracing."""
468	
469	    def run(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> str: ...
470	
471	
472	@contextmanager
473	def _span(name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any], *, kind: str = "AGENT") -> Iterator[None]:
474	    """Emit an OpenTelemetry span if a provider is registered; a cheap no-op otherwise.
475	
476	    Stamps the OpenInference ``openinference.span.kind`` (default ``AGENT`` — a reasoning call)
477	    so Phoenix classifies the span instead of rendering it UNKNOWN. OpenTelemetry is imported
478	    lazily, so the seam traces when the (self-hosted, on-box) observability stack is wired and is
479	    a silent no-op in tests / offline (ADR-0003).
480	    """
481	    try:
482	        from opentelemetry import trace
483	    except ImportError:
484	        yield
485	        return
486	    tracer = trace.get_tracer("assay_engine.reasoning")
487	    with tracer.start_as_current_span(name) as span:
488	        span.set_attribute("openinference.span.kind", kind)
489	        for k, v in attributes.items():
490	            span.set_attribute(k, v)
491	        yield
492	
493	
494	class TieredReasoningSeam:
495	    """Concrete tiered seam. ``run`` returns text; ``run_json`` parses a JSON reply.
496	
497	    Backends and tracing are resolved lazily, so an instance constructs with no external
498	    dependencies; calls fail loud (``PermanentReasoningError``) if a tier's backend or its
499	    subscription auth is absent. When the observability stack is present, each call emits an
500	    OpenTelemetry span; otherwise tracing is a no-op.
501	    """
502	
503	    def run(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
504	        attrs = {"assay.tier": request.tier.value, "assay.purpose": request.purpose}
505	        with _span("reasoning.run", attrs):
506	            return _run_with_retries(request)
507	
508	    def run_json(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> Any:
509	        """Generate and parse JSON, re-rolling on parse failure.
510	
511	        If the caller supplies ``params["json_schema"]``, the BULK tier uses Ollama's
512	        schema-constrained decoding (the reliable path). Otherwise it falls back to loose JSON
513	        mode + prompt instruction. BULK decoding can be deterministic (temp 0), so re-issuing the
514	        identical prompt would yield a byte-identical bad reply; each retry **varies the seed**
515	        (forcing a different deterministic decode) and *monotonically* raises temperature toward
516	        1.0 — never lowering it, so re-rolls never sharpen back toward the same output. ``params``
517	        is frozen, so each attempt builds a fresh request (issue #R6).
518	        """
519	        base_temp = float(request.params.get("temperature", 0.0))
520	        system = request.params.get("system")
521	        json_system = (
522	            f"{system}\n\nRespond with ONLY a single JSON value, no prose."
523	            if system
524	            else "Respond with ONLY a single JSON value, no prose."
525	        )
526	        last: ReasoningError | None = None
527	        with _span(
528	            "reasoning.run_json",
529	            {"assay.tier": request.tier.value, "assay.purpose": request.purpose},
530	        ):
531	            for attempt in range(MAX_RETRIES + 1):
532	                temp = min(1.0, base_temp + 0.2 * attempt)  # monotonic, never decreases (M3)
533	                params = dict(request.params)
534	                params.update(temperature=temp, system=json_system, _json_mode=True, _seed=attempt)
535	                attempt_req = ReasoningRequest(
536	                    prompt=request.prompt,
537	                    tier=request.tier,
538	                    purpose=request.purpose,
539	                    params=params,
540	                )
541	                try:
542	                    return extract_json(_run_with_retries(attempt_req))
543	                except ReasoningError as exc:
544	                    last = exc
545	            raise last if last is not None else ReasoningError("run_json failed")
546	
547	    @staticmethod
548	    def is_available(tier: StakesTier) -> bool:
549	        """Cheap liveness check (spends no LLM turn). Never raises — returns False on error."""
550	        if _truthy("ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING"):
551	            return False
552	        try:
553	            if tier is StakesTier.BULK:
554	                require_loopback_url(BULK_BASE_URL, what="bulk-tier model base URL")
555	                import urllib.request
556	
557	                with urllib.request.urlopen(f"{BULK_BASE_URL}/api/tags", timeout=2) as resp:
558	                    return bool(resp.status == 200)
559	            if tier is StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES:
560	                import shutil
561	
562	                return shutil.which("claude") is not None and _high_stakes_auth_present()
563	        except Exception:
564	            return False
565	        return False
566	
567	
568	class UnconfiguredReasoningSeam:
569	    """Explicit fail-loud seam for contexts that must not perform reasoning."""
570	
571	    def run(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
572	        raise NotImplementedError(
573	            "this context uses an UnconfiguredReasoningSeam — supply a TieredReasoningSeam "
574	            "(or a test double) to perform reasoning"
575	        )
576

1	"""Local vector store seam (ADR-0003) — loopback-enforced Qdrant factory.
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform. The client is constructed only against a
4	loopback host; the guard fires *before* importing the (optional ``persistence`` extra)
5	``qdrant_client``, so a misconfigured remote host is rejected regardless of whether the
6	package is installed.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import os
12	import uuid
13	from typing import Any, Protocol, Sequence, runtime_checkable
14	
15	from assay_engine._local import require_loopback_host
16	
17	VECTOR_HOST = os.environ.get("ASSAY_VECTOR_HOST", "localhost")
18	VECTOR_HTTP_PORT = int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_VECTOR_HTTP_PORT", "6333"))
19	
20	# Stable namespace for mapping the engine's string ids onto Qdrant point UUIDs.
21	_POINT_NS = uuid.UUID("a55a9e00-0000-4000-8000-000000000001")
22	
23	
24	@runtime_checkable
25	class VectorStore(Protocol):
26	    def upsert(self, ids: Sequence[str], vectors: Sequence[Sequence[float]]) -> None: ...
27	    def query(self, vector: Sequence[float], k: int) -> list[tuple[str, float]]: ...
28	
29	
30	def vector_store_url() -> str:
31	    host = require_loopback_host(VECTOR_HOST, what="vector store host")
32	    return f"http://{host}:{VECTOR_HTTP_PORT}"
33	
34	
35	def get_qdrant_client() -> Any:
36	    """Construct a loopback-only Qdrant client (lazy import of the optional extra)."""
37	    host = require_loopback_host(VECTOR_HOST, what="vector store host")  # before any import
38	    try:
39	        from qdrant_client import QdrantClient
40	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
41	        raise RuntimeError(
42	            "vector store requires the 'persistence' extra (qdrant-client) — not installed"
43	        ) from exc
44	    return QdrantClient(host=host, port=VECTOR_HTTP_PORT)
45	
46	
47	def _point_id(ref: str) -> str:
48	    """Deterministic Qdrant point UUID for an engine string id (Qdrant ids must be int/UUID)."""
49	    return str(uuid.uuid5(_POINT_NS, ref))
50	
51	
52	class QdrantVectorStore:
53	    """Reference :class:`VectorStore` over a loopback Qdrant collection (cosine).
54	
55	    The engine's ids are arbitrary strings; Qdrant point ids must be int/UUID, so each id is
56	    mapped to a deterministic UUID and the original id is stored in the point payload (and
57	    returned by ``query``). This is a goal-agnostic reference implementation; a study may
58	    supply its own ``VectorStore`` (collection naming, distance, sharding are study choices).
59	    """
60	
61	    def __init__(self, collection: str, dim: int, *, client: Any | None = None) -> None:
62	        self._collection = collection
63	        self._dim = dim
64	        self._client = client if client is not None else get_qdrant_client()
65	
66	    def ensure_collection(self) -> None:
67	        from qdrant_client.models import Distance, VectorParams
68	
69	        if not self._client.collection_exists(self._collection):
70	            self._client.create_collection(
71	                self._collection,
72	                vectors_config=VectorParams(size=self._dim, distance=Distance.COSINE),
73	            )
74	
75	    def upsert(
76	        self, ids: Sequence[str], vectors: Sequence[Sequence[float]], *, batch_size: int = 256
77	    ) -> None:
78	        from qdrant_client.models import PointStruct
79	
80	        if len(ids) != len(vectors):
81	            raise ValueError("ids and vectors must have equal length")
82	        points = [
83	            PointStruct(id=_point_id(i), vector=list(v), payload={"ref": i})
84	            for i, v in zip(ids, vectors)
85	        ]
86	        # Chunk so a large ingest cannot exceed the server's request payload limit (Qdrant
87	        # guidance: do not upload points one-by-one, but bound the batch size).
88	        for start in range(0, len(points), batch_size):
89	            self._client.upsert(self._collection, points=points[start : start + batch_size])
90	
91	    def query(self, vector: Sequence[float], k: int) -> list[tuple[str, float]]:
92	        res = self._client.query_points(
93	            self._collection, query=list(vector), limit=k, with_payload=True
94	        )
95	        return [(p.payload["ref"], float(p.score)) for p in res.points]
96

1	"""Durable run-state checkpointer — local, data-sovereign (ADR-0003).
2	
3	The orchestration graph persists its full state (current phase, gate decisions, locked
4	hypotheses, verdicts) through a checkpointer so a run can interrupt at a human-approval gate
5	and resume later. That state holds the entire analysis, so the store is **on-box only**
6	(ADR-0003); a SaaS checkpointer is disqualified.
7	
8	This module is the LangGraph checkpointer factory, ported faithfully from the prior
9	platform's hardened implementation and generalized (dataset-agnostic ``ASSAY_*`` env, no
10	config-file coupling — see ADR-0006). The hardened behaviors are deliberately preserved
11	because each prevents a real production failure:
12	
13	- a **self-healing ConnectionPool** (not a raw connection): when Postgres goes away — node
14	  sleep, restart, idle timeout, a network blip — a cached raw connection is permanently
15	  broken and every later checkpoint op raises, i.e. durability is lost exactly during the
16	  interrupt/resume the checkpointer exists to support. The pool runs a liveness check on
17	  checkout and replaces dead connections;
18	- a **session advisory lock** serializing first-time schema setup across *processes*, run on
19	  the *same* connection as the DDL (a pool would otherwise check out a different connection,
20	  leaving the lock ineffective);
21	- a **once-per-process-per-conn guard** (``setup()`` is idempotent but re-locking + replaying
22	  the migration query on every call is wasteful), with an in-process lock making the
23	  check-and-set atomic so concurrent threads don't both run setup;
24	- **credential redaction**: a backend exception (psycopg/psycopg_pool) can embed the full
25	  ``user:password`` URI/DSN in its own message, so the conn string is scrubbed precisely and
26	  the exception text via URI-userinfo + DSN-password backstops; the redacted message is then
27	  raised *outside* the exception handler, so the credential-bearing original is retained as
28	  neither ``__cause__`` nor ``__context__`` (a logger walking ``__context__`` cannot
29	  re-expose it);
30	- a single shared **atexit** pool-cleanup handler (one per process, not one per call) so the
31	  pool's worker thread is joined before interpreter finalization.
32	
33	LangGraph/psycopg are optional (the ``persistence`` extra) and imported lazily; the
34	credential-redaction and connection-resolution logic is pure and unit-tested offline.
35	"""
36	
37	from __future__ import annotations
38	
39	import atexit
40	import os
41	import re
42	import threading
43	from typing import Any, cast
44	from urllib.parse import urlsplit
45	
46	from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
47	
48	_DEFAULT_PG = "postgresql://localhost:5432/assay"
49	
50	# Arbitrary fixed key for the Postgres session advisory lock that serializes checkpoint
51	# schema migrations across concurrent initializers. The value is irrelevant beyond being a
52	# stable per-logical-schema constant; "ASSY" as an int.
53	_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY = 0x4153_5359
54	
55	# Guards the process-local one-time-init bookkeeping. The PG advisory lock serializes DDL
56	# across PROCESSES; this lock makes the in-process check-and-set sequences (atexit
57	# registration, the initialized-conn set) atomic so two threads can't double-register or
58	# double-setup.
59	_init_lock = threading.Lock()
60	
61	# Pools opened by the factory, closed once at process exit via a single shared atexit handler
62	# (registering one handler per call would accumulate handlers across repeated factory calls).
63	_OPEN_POOLS: list[Any] = []
64	_atexit_registered = False
65	
66	# conn_strs whose schema bootstrap (advisory lock + setup() DDL) has already run in this
67	# process — bootstrap once per process per schema; the advisory lock still guards the
68	# first-time cross-process race.
69	_INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS: set[str] = set()
70	
71	# Strips ``://user:password@`` (URI userinfo) from any URI-like substring. '/' is allowed
72	# inside the userinfo so a password containing '/' is still redacted; only '@' (the userinfo
73	# terminator) and whitespace bound the match.
74	_CREDS_RE = re.compile(r"://[^@\s]+@")
75	# Strips a libpq keyword/value DSN password token (``password=...`` / ``pgpassword=...``),
76	# handling quoted values that may contain spaces.
77	_DSN_PW_RE = re.compile(r"(?i)\b(password|pgpassword)\s*=\s*('[^']*'|\"[^\"]*\"|\S+)")
78	
79	
80	def _sanitize_conn_str(conn_str: str) -> str:
81	    """Return ``scheme://host[:port]/path`` with any ``user:password`` userinfo stripped.
82	
83	    Robust to malformed userinfo: a password containing ``/``/``#`` makes ``urlsplit``'s
84	    ``.port`` raise ``ValueError`` on access, so the whole parse (including attribute access)
85	    is guarded and falls back to the generic userinfo-strip regex / a constant placeholder.
86	    """
87	    try:
88	        u = urlsplit(conn_str)
89	        host = u.hostname or ""
90	        port = u.port
91	        scheme = u.scheme
92	        path = u.path
93	    except ValueError:
94	        return _CREDS_RE.sub("://", conn_str)
95	    if not host:
96	        return _CREDS_RE.sub("://", conn_str) if scheme else "<store>"
97	    if ":" in host:  # IPv6 literal — urlsplit drops the brackets; restore them
98	        host = f"[{host}]"
99	    netloc = f"{host}:{port}" if port else host
100	    return f"{scheme}://{netloc}{path}" if scheme else "<store>"
101	
102	
103	def redact_creds(text: str, conn_str: str = "") -> str:
104	    """Remove credentials a backend exception may embed in its message text.
105	
106	    Layers, in order of precision:
107	
108	    1. exact replacement of the known ``conn_str`` (handles any password characters, incl.
109	       ``/`` and spaces) — this is the precise layer and covers the real connection string;
110	    2. a generic URI-userinfo strip (``://user:pass@`` → ``://``) as a backstop for any
111	       *other* URI in the text;
112	    3. a libpq DSN ``password=``/``pgpassword=`` strip for the keyword/value form.
113	
114	    Layers 2–3 are best-effort for *secondary* endpoints (e.g. a replica DSN) the caller did
115	    not pass as ``conn_str``; the primary connection string is always scrubbed precisely.
116	    """
117	    if conn_str:
118	        text = text.replace(conn_str, _sanitize_conn_str(conn_str))
119	    text = _CREDS_RE.sub("://", text)
120	    return _DSN_PW_RE.sub(r"\1=***", text)
121	
122	
123	def get_postgres_connection_string() -> str:
124	    """Resolve the Postgres connection string (``ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL`` env → loopback default).
125	
126	    The env path bypasses any external config validation, so loopback is enforced here at the
127	    point of use (ADR-0003 defense-in-depth): graph state must not leave the box.
128	    """
129	    url = os.environ.get("ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL") or _DEFAULT_PG
130	    return require_local_uri(url, what="checkpointer connection")
131	
132	
133	def _safe_close(pool: Any) -> None:
134	    """Close one pool, swallowing errors so a single failing ``close()`` does not abort
135	    cleanup of the remaining pools at interpreter exit (``close()`` calls a C ``finish()``
136	    that can raise)."""
137	    try:
138	        pool.close()
139	    except Exception:
140	        pass
141	
142	
143	def _close_all_pools() -> None:
144	    for p in _OPEN_POOLS:
145	        _safe_close(p)
146	
147	
148	def _register_pool_cleanup(pool: Any) -> None:
149	    global _atexit_registered
150	    with _init_lock:
151	        _OPEN_POOLS.append(pool)
152	        if not _atexit_registered:
153	            atexit.register(_close_all_pools)
154	            _atexit_registered = True
155	
156	
157	def get_checkpointer(use_memory: bool = False) -> Any:
158	    """Return a configured LangGraph checkpointer.
159	
160	    ``use_memory=True`` returns an in-memory saver (tests / no-Postgres). Otherwise a
161	    Postgres-backed saver over a self-healing pool, with advisory-locked one-time schema
162	    setup. Raises ``RuntimeError`` (with credentials redacted) if the backend is unreachable
163	    or the extra is not installed.
164	    """
165	    try:
166	        if use_memory:
167	            from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver
168	
169	            return MemorySaver()
170	        from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres import PostgresSaver
171	        from psycopg.rows import dict_row
172	        from psycopg_pool import ConnectionPool
173	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
174	        raise RuntimeError(
175	            "Postgres checkpointer requires the 'persistence' extra "
176	            "(langgraph-checkpoint-postgres, psycopg[binary,pool]) — not installed"
177	        ) from exc
178	
179	    conn_str = get_postgres_connection_string()
180	    failure: str | None = None
181	    try:
182	        pool = ConnectionPool(
183	            conn_str,
184	            min_size=1,
185	            max_size=8,
186	            kwargs={"autocommit": True, "prepare_threshold": 0, "row_factory": dict_row},
187	            check=ConnectionPool.check_connection,  # liveness check on checkout (self-healing)
188	            open=True,
189	        )
190	        _register_pool_cleanup(pool)
191	        # Bootstrap the schema once per process per conn_str. The advisory lock and the DDL
192	        # must run on the SAME connection (pg_advisory_lock is session-scoped); a pool-backed
193	        # setup() would lock one connection and DDL on another, leaving the lock ineffective.
194	        with _init_lock:
195	            if conn_str not in _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS:
196	                with pool.connection() as conn:
197	                    conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_lock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
198	                    try:
199	                        # row_factory=dict_row makes this connection dict-rowed at runtime
200	                        # (what PostgresSaver requires); the cast asserts that runtime truth
201	                        # the type system can't infer from the pool kwargs.
202	                        PostgresSaver(cast(Any, conn)).setup()  # DDL on the locked connection
203	                    finally:
204	                        # Best-effort unlock; the lock auto-releases when the connection
205	                        # returns to the pool, and raising here would mask a setup() error.
206	                        try:
207	                            conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_unlock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
208	                        except Exception:
209	                            pass
210	                _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS.add(conn_str)
211	        return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, pool))  # dict-rowed pool (row_factory=dict_row)
212	    except Exception as exc:
213	        # Build the redacted message here, but raise it OUTSIDE the handler (below) so the
214	        # original — credential-bearing — exception is retained as neither __cause__ NOR
215	        # __context__ (a logger walking __context__ would otherwise re-expose the password).
216	        failure = (
217	            f"Postgres checkpointer connection failed (is Postgres running on "
218	            f"{_sanitize_conn_str(conn_str)}?): {redact_creds(str(exc), conn_str)}. "
219	            "For tests without Postgres, use get_checkpointer(use_memory=True)."
220	        )
221	    raise RuntimeError(failure)
222	
223	
224	def configured_checkpointer(use_memory: bool | None = None) -> Any:
225	    """Return the checkpointer the environment selects.
226	
227	    ``use_memory`` (or ``ASSAY_CHECKPOINT_BACKEND=memory``) chooses the in-memory saver;
228	    otherwise Postgres.
229	    """
230	    if use_memory is None:
231	        use_memory = os.environ.get("ASSAY_CHECKPOINT_BACKEND", "postgres").lower() == "memory"
232	    return get_checkpointer(use_memory=use_memory)
233

1	"""Self-hosted tracing — OpenTelemetry → a local collector (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's Phoenix/OTel bootstrap. Everything is
4	on-box: spans export over OTLP to a loopback-bound collector; nothing leaves the machine.
5	OpenTelemetry and the Phoenix collector are optional (the ``observability`` extra) and
6	imported lazily, so this module loads — and the engine runs untraced — with neither present.
7	
8	The reasoning seam already emits spans through the global OTel tracer provider; calling
9	:func:`bootstrap_tracing` once at startup wires that global provider at a local collector so
10	those spans land somewhere. Without the extra (or with ``ASSAY_DISABLE_TRACING`` set),
11	tracing degrades to a silent no-op.
12	"""
13	
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import atexit
17	import logging
18	import os
19	import signal
20	import threading
21	from contextlib import contextmanager
22	from typing import Any, Iterator, Mapping, Protocol, runtime_checkable
23	
24	from assay_engine._local import require_loopback_host
25	
26	_log = logging.getLogger("assay_engine.observability")
27	
28	# Local collector endpoint (loopback-enforced).
29	TRACING_HOST = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_HOST", "localhost")
30	TRACING_PORT = int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_PORT", "6006"))
31	PROJECT_NAME = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_PROJECT", "assay")
32	
33	_provider: Any = None
34	# Guards the _provider check-then-set so concurrent bootstrap_tracing() calls don't race.
35	_provider_lock = threading.Lock()
36	
37	
38	def _disabled() -> bool:
39	    return os.environ.get("ASSAY_DISABLE_TRACING", "").strip().lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}
40	
41	
42	def tracing_base_url() -> str:
43	    require_loopback_host(TRACING_HOST, what="tracing collector host")
44	    return f"http://{TRACING_HOST}:{TRACING_PORT}"
45	
46	
47	def tracing_endpoint() -> str:
48	    """OTLP/HTTP traces endpoint at the local collector (loopback-enforced)."""
49	    return f"{tracing_base_url()}/v1/traces"
50	
51	
52	def bootstrap_tracing() -> Any:
53	    """Idempotently wire the global OTel tracer provider to the local collector.
54	
55	    Returns the provider, or ``None`` if tracing is disabled, the extra is not installed, or
56	    setup fails — tracing must never be load-bearing, so this degrades gracefully.
57	    """
58	    global _provider
59	    if _disabled():
60	        return None
61	    # Lock the check-then-set so two threads can't both register the global provider.
62	    with _provider_lock:
63	        if _provider is not None:
64	            return _provider
65	        try:
66	            require_loopback_host(TRACING_HOST, what="tracing collector host")
67	            # Bound the OTLP export so a downed collector can never hang teardown (#O3).
68	            os.environ.setdefault("OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_TIMEOUT", "2")
69	            from phoenix.otel import register
70	
71	            provider = register(
72	                project_name=PROJECT_NAME,
73	                endpoint=tracing_endpoint(),
74	                protocol="http/protobuf",  # pin: endpoint is .../v1/traces — never flip to gRPC
75	                set_global_tracer_provider=True,
76	                batch=True,  # async BatchSpanProcessor — never block the run on export (#O2)
77	                verbose=False,  # no stdout banner from a library context
78	            )
79	            _provider = provider
80	            _warn_if_not_global(provider)
81	            _instrument_langchain()
82	            _install_flush_on_exit(provider)
83	            return _provider
84	        except Exception:
85	            # Missing extra / collector down / registration race — run untraced.
86	            return None
87	
88	
89	def _warn_if_not_global(provider: Any) -> None:
90	    """If another provider already won the global slot, our manual spans would vanish (#O6)."""
91	    try:
92	        from opentelemetry import trace
93	
94	        if trace.get_tracer_provider() is not provider:
95	            _log.warning(
96	                "assay tracing: another OpenTelemetry provider holds the global slot; "
97	                "manually-emitted spans may not reach the local collector"
98	            )
99	    except Exception:
100	        pass
101	
102	
103	def _instrument_langchain() -> None:
104	    """Capture LangChain auto-spans (the bulk reasoning tier uses LangChain) — best-effort (#O5)."""
105	    try:
106	        from openinference.instrumentation.langchain import LangChainInstrumentor
107	
108	        LangChainInstrumentor().instrument(skip_dep_check=True)
109	    except Exception:
110	        pass
111	
112	
113	def _install_flush_on_exit(provider: Any) -> None:
114	    """Flush buffered spans on clean exit (atexit) and on SIGTERM (#O3).
115	
116	    atexit does not run on ``kill``/container-stop, so a main-thread SIGTERM handler also
117	    flushes+shuts down. Both are guarded — tracing teardown must never raise.
118	    """
119	    flush = getattr(provider, "force_flush", None)
120	    if not callable(flush):
121	        return
122	    atexit.register(lambda: _safe(flush))
123	    if threading.current_thread() is threading.main_thread():
124	        try:
125	            prior = signal.getsignal(signal.SIGTERM)
126	
127	            def _handler(signum: int, frame: Any) -> None:
128	                _safe(flush)
129	                _safe(getattr(provider, "shutdown", lambda: None))
130	                if callable(prior):
131	                    prior(signum, frame)
132	
133	            signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handler)
134	        except (ValueError, OSError):  # pragma: no cover - not on main thread / unsupported
135	            pass
136	
137	
138	def _safe(fn: Any) -> None:
139	    try:
140	        fn()
141	    except Exception:
142	        pass
143	
144	
145	@contextmanager
146	def run_trace_context(run_id: str | None) -> Iterator[None]:
147	    """Stamp ``run_id`` onto every span emitted inside the block (cross-store correlation).
148	
149	    A no-op when ``run_id`` is None or OpenTelemetry is absent.
150	    """
151	    if run_id is None:
152	        yield
153	        return
154	    try:
155	        from opentelemetry import baggage, context
156	    except ImportError:
157	        yield
158	        return
159	    token = context.attach(baggage.set_baggage("assay.run_id", run_id))
160	    try:
161	        yield
162	    finally:
163	        context.detach(token)
164	
165	
166	# OpenInference requires every span carry a span kind so Phoenix classifies it (CHAIN/LLM/
167	# AGENT/TOOL); a plain OTel span with no kind renders as UNKNOWN in the Phoenix UI. We set the
168	# raw semantic-convention attribute directly (no openinference import needed for a manual span).
169	_OPENINFERENCE_SPAN_KIND = "openinference.span.kind"
170	
171	
172	@runtime_checkable
173	class Tracer(Protocol):
174	    @contextmanager
175	    def span(
176	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
177	    ) -> Iterator[None]: ...
178	
179	
180	class OtelTracer:
181	    """A tracer that emits OpenTelemetry spans via the global provider (no-op if OTel absent).
182	
183	    Use this where a component wants explicit spans rather than relying on auto-instrumentation;
184	    it honors whatever provider :func:`bootstrap_tracing` registered. Each span is stamped with
185	    its OpenInference ``kind`` so Phoenix classifies it (default ``CHAIN``).
186	    """
187	
188	    @contextmanager
189	    def span(
190	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
191	    ) -> Iterator[None]:
192	        try:
193	            from opentelemetry import trace
194	        except ImportError:
195	            yield
196	            return
197	        tracer = trace.get_tracer("assay_engine")
198	        with tracer.start_as_current_span(name) as sp:
199	            sp.set_attribute(_OPENINFERENCE_SPAN_KIND, kind)
200	            for k, v in (attributes or {}).items():
201	                sp.set_attribute(k, v)
202	            yield
203	
204	
205	class UnconfiguredTracer:
206	    """Fail-loud placeholder for contexts that must not silently drop spans."""
207	
208	    @contextmanager
209	    def span(
210	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
211	    ) -> Iterator[None]:
212	        raise NotImplementedError(
213	            "no tracer configured — call bootstrap_tracing() and use OtelTracer, or supply a "
214	            "test double"
215	        )
216	        yield  # pragma: no cover - keeps this a generator/contextmanager
217
